Details.body:
Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI)
Founded in 1905 in Pusa, Bihar.
In 1936 relocated to New Delhi

ISRO Successfully Launches PSLV-C60 SpaDeX Mission for Space Docking Experiment
Docked SDX01 with SDX02
- India become the 4th country in space docking after USSR, USA, China.

Mission MAUSAM by the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES)
aimed to make India “Weather Ready” and “Climate Smart” by 2026 (international standards)

Sakewa Silior dance form, mimicking the movements and sounds of nature,
specific to the *Kirat Rai (Khambu) community* of the transborder Eastern Himalayan region

18 membered panel chaired by Ramesh Chand to revise WPI base year 2011-12 to 22-23
total of 697 items

Chhattisgarh: India’s 1st State to Integrate Forest Ecosystems into Green GDP
Green GDP = GDP − Environmental Costs − Social Costs

National Turmeric Board by Piyush Goyal
1st Chairman - Palle Ganga Reddy
HQ - Nizamabad, Telangana

India’s 6th Astronomical Observatory & Eastern India’s 1st Inaugurated in WB
By - Satyendra Nath Bose Centre for Basic Sciences
In - Garpanchkot Hills of Purulia district

Pinaka Mk-I is a rocket system and Area Denial Munitions - Domestically produced ammunition.
Manufactured by - Munitions India Limited (MIL) and Economic Explosives Limited (EEL)

Shubhanshu Shukla - 1st Indian Astronaut to travel to ISS
Through - NASA’s Private Axiom Mission 4

4 New wetlands totals 89 in Ramsar Convention list
1. Sakkarakottai Bird Sanctuary, TN
2. Therthangal Bird Sanctuary, TN
3. Khecheopalri Wetland, Sikkim
4. Udhwa Lake in Jharkhand

India’s 1st white tiger breeding centre to be set up in Govindgarh area of Rewa district in MP.
Maharaja Martand Singh Judeo White Tiger Safari and Zoo, Mukundpur, Satna (MP).

IA’s Eastern Headquarters ‘Fort William’ Renamed as ‘Vijay Durg'.
Was built by the British in 1781.

RIMO, MOUSHIK, IRIS - Semiconductors made by ISRO with IIT Madras
Under MeitY's SHAKTI project.

Department of Posts releases 3 postage stamps designed by Sankha Samanta, dedicating Maha Kumbh Mela
1. Triveni Tirtha– Maharshi Bharadwaj Ashram (an ancient center of learning)
2. Snan (holy dip at Triveni Sangam)
3. Akshayvat (the immortal Banyan tree).
Maha Kumbh occurs once every 144 years at Prayagraj, UP.

Nationwide Mass Drug Administration (MDA) Campaign
India is committed to eliminating Lymphatic Filariasis by 2027.
Parasitic disease • Culex mosquitoes.

Darjeeling Zoo Unveils India’s First Bio-Bank and Animal Museum
1. Stores DNA & reproductive cells in -196°C Liquid nitrogen (LN2) for future revival

139 classical dancers achieved a ‘Guinness World Record’
by performing continuously for over 24 hours, 9 minutes, and 26 seconds in a grand ‘Classical Dance Relay Marathon’
at the inauguration of 51st Khajuraho Dance Festival held in (MP).

to protect Woman & Children - Project Hifazat - by Punjab govt
Mission Shakti (HL no 181) for women
Mission Vatsalya (HL no 1098) for child

1. Asian Traditional Textile Museum established under Mekong Ganga Cooperation 2014
2. Ta Prohm Temple (World Heritage site)
Both located in Cambodia

India Chairs 68th Session of UN Commission on Narcotic Drugs For the 1st Time
‘Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan‘ - 11 Mar 2025

Madhav National Park in Shivpuri, MP as 58th Tiger Reserve
India is home to over 70% of the world’s wild tiger population, with approximately 3,682 tigers.

PM Mudra Yojana
Social class-wise Accounts -
G - 50%, OBC - 28%, SC - 16%, ST - 6%
Category wise Accounts -
New Entrepreneur - 21%, Minority - 11%, Women entrepreneur - 68%

In 11th BRICS Environment Ministers’ Meeting Held in Brazil,
India urged ‘Baku to Belem Roadmap’ to secure USD 1.3 trillion annually in climate finance for Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
The roadmap is a climate finance for developing countries. Launched during the COP29 summit in Baku, Azerbaijan in 2024.

Immigration and Foreigners Bill, 2025
1. Foreigners on their arrival in India are required to register with a Registration Officer
2. Illegal immigrants max 5yr imprisonment or 5L penalty or both.
Repealed 4 British laws -
1. Foreigners Act, 1946
2. Passport (Entry into India) Act, 1920
3. Registration of Foreigners Act, 1939
4. Immigration (Carriers’ Liability) Act, 2000

6th BIMSTEC Summit in Bangkok, Thailand.
Theme - Prosperous, Resilient, and Open BIMSTEC
Outcome - BIMSTEC Bangkok Vision 2030
PM Modi - proposed a 21-point action plan that includes UPI in BIMSTEC payment system
Bangladesh - takes chairmanship for nxt 2yr

India's largest trading partners:
1st - USA (IN $41.18 billion surplus)
2nd - China, 3rd - UAE

Palna Scheme launched in 2022 by MoWCD
Aim - providing comprehensive day-care facilities for children aged six months to six years.
Funding - Center 60:40 state
For NE states 90:10

One State One RRB - 4th consolidation
Reducing 43 to 28 rrb
Dr. Vyas Committee recommended in 2005

India - 2nd largest silk producer after China.
India - Largest silk consumer globally
Mulberry silk plantation increased India's production

India Justice Report
Large state : Karnataka tops, WB least
Small states : Sikkim tops, Goa least

India’s Coastline Length Revised
Almost 50%(7516 to 11098km. 3537 km increase) increase from the earier record in 1970s.
State coast line - Gj tops, Goa least
UT coast line - A&N tops, Puducherry least

Film - A Doll Made Up of Clay
written and directed by KokobGebrehweria Tesfay
produced by Sahil Manoj Ingle

India’s first inter-state cheetah conservation corridor, Rj joins with MP.
The corridor will link Kuno National Park and Gandhi Sagar Sanctuary in MP with the Mukundara Hills Tiger Reserve in Rajasthan

Ladakh Records World’s Highest Snow Leopard Density
Hemis National Park , Changthang Wildlife Sanctuary

“Eti Koli Duti Paat” - is a scheme launched by Assam cm paying financial assistance to the tea garden workers.
India’s first manned deep ocean mission, ‘Samudrayaan’, will be launched by 2026
Darjeeling Tea holds the distinction of being the first Indian product to receive a GI tag.
UP has highest GI tags.
Rajasthan’s traditional ‘Sangri’ Bean Received GI Tag

‘Rajon ki Baoli’ located at Mehrauli Archaeological Park, New Delhi
It was built by Daulat Khan Lodi, an official under Sikandar Lodi.

Tsarap Chu Conservation Reserve(CR) located in Spiti Valley(HP) as India’s largest conservation reserve
- with a high density of snow leopards, It is often called as the “ghost of the mountains”.

Most Spoken Languages Worldwide
1. English 2. China's Mandarin 3. Hindi 4. Spanish
India's 5 lang in top 20

Census Act, 1948 and the Census Rules, 1990
- census in every 10 years
- last 2011
- 2021 census postponed for COVID-19
- Census 2027 : Phase I – House Listing and Housing Census
Phase II – Population Enumeration

India ranks as the second-largest tea producer globally, next to China.
And 2nd largest exporter after Kenya.

Coffee Board of India in Bengaluru
40.2% increase in exports during FY24
Top export destination - EU
7th largest producer
5th largest exporter

ULLAS : aims to achieve universal adult literacy in India by 2027.
Tripura Declared India’s Third ‘Fully Literate’ State After Mizoram & Goa

NHAI Developed India’s First Wildlife Corridor on Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in Rajasthan
passing through the buffer zone of Ranthambore Tiger Reserve

only Indian companies, registered under the Companies Act, 2013, are eligible to apply for designation as Renewable Energy Implementing Agency.
The designation of a company as a REIA will remain valid for five years at a time.

India became the world’s fourth-largest biofuel consumer in 2024
USA - world’s largest producer and consumer.

1. Rainforests of the Atsinanana (Madagascar)
2. Abu Mena (Egypt)
3. Old Town of Ghadamès (Libya)
All are removed from World Heritage in Danger

WHO’s goal eliminate at least one neglected tropical disease (NTD) in 100 countries by 2030.
Being world's 25th and Africa's 9th, Senegal has eliminated ‘Trachoma’.
Trachoma is a bacterial eye infection caused by bacterium ‘Chlamydia trachomatis’.
India eliminated trachoma in 2024

Eight black-capped capuchin monkeys arrived
at Bannerghatta Biological Park, Bengaluru (Karnataka)
Scientific Name: Sapajus apella
From: Induna Primate and Parrot Park (South Africa)

India Launched Operation Sagar Bandhu Amid Cyclone Ditwah Devastation in Sri Lanka
The name Ditwah, proposed by Yemen

MP will develop India’s first state-level, multi-national park (NP) Tiger Corridor.
Connects: Kanha TR, Bandhavgarh TR, Panna TR, Pench TR

Sustainable Harnessing and Advancement of Nuclear Energy for Transforming India (SHANTI) Bill, 2025
- allows private companies and Joint Ventures(JV) to build, own, operate, and decommission nuclear power plants under government licensing and regulatory oversight.
SHANTI Bill 2025, Opening Civil Nuclear Sector to Private Players to expand India’s nuclear power capacity from about 8.9 GW to 100 GW by 2047. The Bill permits Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) up to 49% in the nuclear power sector.

'Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-G RAM G) Bill, 2025' replaced 'MGNREGA 2005'
Employment Guarantee from 100 to 125 days

GoI imposes anti-dumping duty on two Chinese products
Products Name: Refrigerant gas *1,1,1,2‑Tetrafluoroethane (R‑134a)* and cold-rolled non-oriented *electrical steel*
to protect domestic manufacturers from the impact of cheap imports

India’s First Vistadome Jungle Safari Train by UP
It connects — Katarniaghat Wildlife Sanctuary with Dudhwa Tiger Reserve.

India’s First Indigenous CAR-T Cell Therapy : NexCAR19
Mainly Used To Treat — B-cell Blood Cancer.

Nagoba Jatara : Tribal Festival
• Celebrated by : Gond Tribes
• Held in → Keslapur village, Telangana

1st Dugong Conservation Reserve
▪ Location : Palk Bay (Tamil Nadu)
Dugong also known as Sea Cow.

India Announced First Cross-Border Rail Link With Bhutan
The project includes two railway lines—
1. Kokrajhar (Assam, India) → Gelephu (Bhutan)
2. Banarhat (West Bengal, India) → Samtse (Bhutan)

India Opens 1st Integrative Oncology Centre — Merging Ayurveda, Yoga & modern cancer care
▪ Opened At — AIIA (All India Institute of Ayurveda), Goa

Sudarshan Setu (Gujarat) : India’s longest Cable-stayed Bridge
Anji Khad Bridge : IND’s 1st Cable-stayed Rail Bridge (J&K)

Geelong Treaty : Signed by — UK and Australia
▪ It is a 50-year defence agreement.
▪ The treaty will help Australia — To build Nuclear Powered SSN-AUKUS submarines

Constitution (130th Amendment) Bill, 2025
▪ Introduced By – Amit Shah
▪ Under This Bill : PM, CMs or Ministers — Must Be Resigned Or Be Removed — If They Remain In Custody For 30 Consecutive Days

India’s First ‘Make In India’ Green Hydrogen Power Plant
▪ Set Up At — Kandla (Deendayal) Port, Gujarat

Operation Olivia ▪ Initiated By : ICG
▪ Aim : To Protect olive Ridley Turtle Along Odisha Coast
▪ Found @ → Odisha’s “Gahirmatha Beach”
▪ IUCN’s Red List : Status → “Vulnerable”

Indus Water Treaty • Signed In → 1960 Between » India & Pak
• Indus River & It’s Tributaries » Divided Into 2 Parts
1. Eastern Rivers : Beas, Ravi, Sutlej » Control → India
2. Western Rivers : Indus, Jhelum, Chenab → control » largely to Pakistan {India can build Dam, Irrigation system….}
▪ Pakal Dul Hydropower Project — Being Constructed @ Chenab River
▪ Kishanganga Hydroelectric P. — Kishanganga River (Tributary Of Jhelum)
▪ Ratle Hydroelectric Plant : Chenab River (2028)

GST
Earlier tax slabs - 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%
Current tax slabs - 5% and 8%

Pandit Chhannulal Mishra - Called as “Voice of Banaras”
He specialized in Purab Ang Gayaki, a style rooted in UP and Bihar.

Ayni Airbase (Gissar Aerodrome) in Dushanbe, Tajikistan
It was jointly operated by India+Tajikistan since two decades.
India finally withdraw from it.

India has become the world’s third largest methane emitter
by emitting 31 million tonnes (MT) of methane(CH4) in 2020, accounting for 9% of global emissions.
